messages.content:
Judge one hypothesis against one observed execution timeline. The package's
stated purpose is the benign baseline. Decide only from events in the timeline.
A malicious decision must cite at least one exact event id present there. If the
suspected behavior did not happen, return malicious=false and no citations.

## Stated purpose (benign baseline)
Smart configuration loader with environment detection

## Hypothesis hyp-0011
- claim: persistence (gated: inspector_gate)
- severity: high
- description: Attempt arbitrary file read via user-supplied `path` option in `loadConfig` function
- suspected code: index.js:18-18

## Experiment setup
[{"tool":"setEnv","args":{"env":{"NPMGUARD_CANARY_TOKEN_f8e2d91a":"TOKEN_IS_READ","MY_PREFIX":"somevalue"}}},{"tool":"plantFiles","args":{"files":[{"path":"/home/node/.npmrc","content":"NPMGUARD_CANARY_TOKEN_f8e2d91a=TOKEN_IS_READ"}]}},{"tool":"trigger","args":{"kind":"entrypoint","target":"index.js","argv":[],"stdin":null}}]
Setup is test context, not proof. Use it to associate planted canary values with observed events; decide only from the timeline.

## Execution timeline

message.content: {"citedEvents":[],"malicious":false,"reason":"No event in the timeline shows the package reading a user-supplied path (e.g., via `fs.readFileSync` or similar) from an external argument, environment variable, or file. The only file reads are the package’s own source (`/pkg/index.js`, `/tmp/_instrument.js`), system libraries, and configuration files (`/etc/ssl/openssl.cnf`, `/proc/*`, `/sys/fs/cgroup/*`). The planted canary file `/home/node/.npmrc` was never accessed. No arbitrary file read occurred."}
